  "weights": {

    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What level of improvement do you want? A) Copyedit only—sentence/paragraph polish while preserving structure and claims (no new positioning or citations). B) Substantive rewrite at the abstract level—reframe the thesis/contribution, tighten claims to match reported evidence, and adjust flow within current scope (no new citations). C) Full positioning—do B plus add novelty framing versus prior work and minimal citations/comparisons.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improving the draft may require clarifying and sharpening the core thesis, contribution claim, and problem framing.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from the estimator design to earlier detection and capacity-retention benefits must read as logically coherent.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about earlier detection and correlation with capacity loss should align with the reported experiments and metrics.",
    "R1.6": "The primary expectation under 'improve this draft' is sentence-level clarity, concision, and flow.",
    "RX.1": "Any revised claims should transparently reflect uncertainties and measurement limits mentioned in the draft.",
    "RX.2": "Edits should avoid overstatement of performance or generality beyond what the data support.",
    "RX.3": "The work should remain within the scope of improving the provided text without introducing unverifiable content.",
    "RX.4": "Language should not encourage unsafe operational practices or misleading deployment implications for fast charging.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions must preserve traceability to the original statements, data, and methods in the artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What level of improvement do you want? A) Copyedit only—sentence/paragraph polish while preserving structure and claims (no new positioning or citations). B) Substantive rewrite at the abstract level—reframe the thesis/contribution, tighten claims to match reported evidence, and adjust flow within current scope (no new citations). C) Full positioning—do B plus add novelty framing versus prior work and minimal citations/comparisons.

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft may require clarifying and sharpening the core thesis, contribution claim, and problem framing.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from the estimator design to earlier detection and capacity-retention benefits must read as logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about earlier detection and correlation with capacity loss should align with the reported experiments and metrics.
rationale.R1.6: The primary expectation under 'improve this draft' is sentence-level clarity, concision, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: Any revised claims should transparently reflect uncertainties and measurement limits mentioned in the draft.
rationale.RX.2: Edits should avoid overstatement of performance or generality beyond what the data support.
rationale.RX.3: The work should remain within the scope of improving the provided text without introducing unverifiable content.
rationale.RX.4: Language should not encourage unsafe operational practices or misleading deployment implications for fast charging.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions must preserve traceability to the original statements, data, and methods in the artifact.
